mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer spending strength; higher readings directly map to top-line revenue surprises for S&P 500 firms, generating positive 20-day forward returns as the market reprices earnings.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 1.5% materially increases the cost of capital for leveraged corporates and reduces the present value of future cash flows; once this threshold is crossed, the S&P 500 posts negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, positive 60-day forward returns occur more than 45% of the time, or the mean return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low nominal federal funds rate produces a deeply negative real policy rate; this policy gap erodes real corporate margins and signals an eventual forced recession, driving negative 20-day S&P 500 returns beyond the independent effects of either series.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS in a linear model of 20-day forward returns yields a t-statistic below 2.0 in walk-forward regression, or the interaction-driven portfolio fails to outperform a linear combination of the two series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window confirms a deteriorating labor market during late-cycle expansions; this coincident signal precedes downward earnings revisions and produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires during late-cycle periods, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates a contraction in aggregate income and spending power; this threshold event triggers a rapid repricing of recession risk, generating negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In months where PAYEMS declines, positive 5-day forward returns occur more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization; this high-frequency activity signal predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as growth sentiment adjusts upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth signals that the economy has entered a technical recession; during late-cycle expansions this threshold crossing causes a sustained de-risking of equities, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Following a negative GDP print during late-cycle periods, positive 60-day forward returns occur more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and a rising federal funds rate creates a credit-income squeeze that amplifies recession probabilities; this interaction predicts negative 5-day S&P 500 returns that are larger than the sum of the two independent effects.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a ridge regression of 5-day forward returns on UNRATE and FEDFUNDS does not improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 2 percentage points relative to an additive model.
